notes: The MXT-1000 is powered exclusively via PoE (802.3af, Class 3) through its single RJ-45 port — there is no separate power inlet. The AMX manufacturer product page (amx.com/en-US/products/mxt-1000) returned HTTP 403, so specifications were drawn from the ManualsLib operation/reference manual (44 pages). The manual overview explicitly states '3 USB host 2.0, Type A ports and 1 Micro-USB device port'; the connector-location section (page 12) describes 'two USB ports for peripherals' on the rear right corner. The entry uses the specification-table count of 3. The Micro-USB port is labeled as a device/output port for camera video and microphone data. Internal components (HD 720P camera, built-in microphone, built-in 2W speaker, NFC transceiver) have no external connector ports. No RS-232, IR, audio jacks, or other physical connectors are present. The MXT-1000-NC variant omits camera, microphone, and NFC. This is a desktop tabletop unit, not rack-mountable.

ports.name: Ethernet / PoE
ports.note: 10/100 Base-T; also delivers PoE 802.3af Class 3 power (up to 8 W max)

ports.name: USB Host
ports.note: 3x USB 2.0 Type-A host ports for peripherals (keyboard, mouse) and firmware updates; the manual overview states '3 USB host 2.0, Type A ports,' though the connector-location section (page 12) describes two rear-accessible USB ports for peripherals

ports.name: Micro-USB
ports.note: 5-pin Micro-USB device port; used for external camera video output (landscape only) and microphone audio output
